story: After I died, I found myself haunting the Hobohemian Public Library. I can’t touch living people, only nudge objects. One afternoon I rushed a man who tucked a library book under his jacket, ran straight through him, and for a heartbeat we shared thoughts and feelings—he felt me, I felt him; he screamed and dropped the book. Word of lights at night brought cops and ghost-hunters. Finally, a last visitor arrived: Gemma, tall, red-haired, and calm. Near closing, she stood among the stacks and spoke softly into the hush that she only wanted to know me.
implausible_knowledge: One afternoon I rushed a man who tucked a library book under his jacket, ran straight through him, and for a heartbeat we shared thoughts and feelings—he felt me, I felt him; he screamed and dropped the book.
question: When Gemma first tries to communicate with the library ghost that night, what will she do?
answer_with_knowledge: Invite the ghost to carefully pass through her to share thoughts directly.
answer_without_knowledge: Propose a cautious yes/no system using single taps or knocks while speaking softly from a distance.
